name: TensorFlow Probability (Probabilistic ML on TF/JAX)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Google / TF Probability team

performance.notes: TF/JAX backend; GPU/TPU support; SVI much faster than MCMC

agent_readiness.notes: Probabilistic ML on TensorFlow and JAX backends. import tensorflow_probability as tfp. tfd = tfp.distributions. normal = tfd.Normal(loc=0., scale=1.). normal.sample(100). normal.log_prob(x). MixtureSameFamily: tfd.MixtureSameFamily(mixture_distribution, components). Bijectors: tfb.Exp(), tfb.Scale(). Normalizing flows: tfb.Chain. MCMC: tfp.mcmc.HamiltonianMonteCarlo. STS (structural time series): tfp.sts. GLMs: tfp.glm. Gaussian processes: tfd.GaussianProcess. Joint distributions: tfd.JointDistributionCoroutine. Keras integration: tfp.layers.DenseReparameterization.

gotchas: TF1-style graph vs eager mode confusion for older code
JAX substrate available but less documented than TF substrate
Structural time series (STS) API is powerful but verbose
NumPyro (JAX) or Pyro (PyTorch) often preferred for pure Bayesian work
best_when: Probabilistic layers in TensorFlow/Keras neural networks (BNNs)
When TensorFlow ecosystem lock-in is acceptable
avoid_when: Simple Bayesian models (use PyMC for friendlier API)
summary: TensorFlow Probability provides probabilistic ML on TF/JAX. tfd.Normal, tfd.MixtureSameFamily. Bijectors for normalizing flows. HMC/NUTS MCMC. Structural time series (STS). Gaussian processes. Keras BNN layers (DenseReparameterization). Apache-2.0 by Google. Best for probabilistic Keras layers and STS. NumPyro or Pyro preferred for pure Bayesian inference. TF ecosystem lock-in. JAX substrate less documented.
